content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 7, 2003

Douglas P. Haubert
Aleshire & Wynder, LLP
18881 Von Karman Avenue, Suite 400
Irvine, CA 92612

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-172

Dear Mr. Haubert:

This letter confirms the receipt of your July 30, 2003, request for advice regarding an unnamed failed candidate for elective office. You indicate you serve as a city attorney for various charter cities and as counsel to city clerks and public officials subject to election contests. While the Commission may provide informal assistance (see regulation 18329(c)(1)), you do not identify the person or persons you are authorized to represent, nor whether you are seeking advice in the capacity of city attorney with a duty to advise, or private counsel. Under the provisions of California Code of Regulations 18329, unless you are an authorized representative of the person for whom you seek assistance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B) and (c)(1).) Moreover, your questions appear to be of a hypothetical nature. (Regulation 18329(c)(4)(D).) Therefore, your request is withdrawn. I enclose a copy of regulation 18329 for your review.

Should you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please contact the Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

Enclosure
JWW:jg
